channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Three men, including husband of Labour MP Joani Reid, released on bail after arrests for suspected assistance to Chinese intelligence
item.description: Three men have been released on bail by the Metropolitan Police after being arrested on suspicion of assisting a foreign intelligence service, specifically linked to China, under Section 3 of the Nati...

item.title: Developments in US-Iran conflict as Iranian warship sunk and regional tensions rise
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate following the US sinking of an Iranian warship. Australian sailors were reportedly on board the submarine involved in the strike. In response, Iran has...

item.title: ADIAfrica Completes Free Eye Screening Program for Over 300 Residents in Rivers State
item.description: The Africa Global Development For Positive Change Initiative (ADIAfrica), together with Stephen Igwe and Judith Igwe International Educational System, don wrap up free eye screening outreach for peopl...

item.title: Taranjit Singh Sandhu appointed as Delhi's Lieutenant Governor following Vinai Kumar Saxena's transfer
item.description: Former Indian diplomat Taranjit Singh Sandhu has been appointed as the new Lieutenant Governor of Delhi, taking over from Vinai Kumar Saxena. The announcement was made on Thursday by Rashtrapati Bhava...

item.title: Court of Appeal upholds death sentence for man convicted of killing infant neighbour
item.description: In Arusha, the Court of Appeal has confirmed the death sentence for Mr Tluway Shauri who was convicted of murdering his neighbour’s three-month-old baby, John Reginald. The tragedy occurred after a qu...

item.title: Interfaith Tamar Campaign Significantly Reduces Gender-Based Violence in Eastern DRC
item.description: For many years, the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) don suffer plenty violence against women, especially after the Second Congo War where rape become weapon and former child soldiers carry toxic id...

item.title: Minor Girl Brutally Assaulted in Bannu Over Wearing Boys' Clothing and Socialising with Boy
item.description: In a distressing case from Bannu district, a young minor girl faced severe violence in the Zairki area of Domail tehsil. A group of armed men reportedly abducted her before subjecting her to a brutal ...

item.title: Interior Secretary to discuss ending hazing practices with Tau Gamma Phi leaders
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla expressed deep concern over the recent death of a freshman maritime student in Cavite due to hazing and announced his plan to meet with senior o...

item.title: Mukuru kwa Njenga Residents Organise Mini Harambee to Compensate Tea Vendor After Young Man Accidentally Breaks Her Thermos
item.description: In the early morning at Mukuru kwa Njenga slums in Nairobi, a young man accidentally knocked over a tea vendor's thermos with his leg while moving aside, causing all the milk to spill and breaking the...

item.title: Court Appearance of Chatunga Mugabe and Co-Accused Delayed Due to Power Outage in Gauteng
item.description: A power outage at the Alexandra Magistrate's Court in Gauteng delayed the proceedings involving Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe and Tobias Matonhodze. The two suspects made a brief appearance in court on T...

item.title: KSE-100 Sees Strong Recovery, Gains Over 5,400 Points
item.description: Pakistan’s stock market witnessed a strong recovery on Thursday as the KSE-100 index closed at 161,210.67 points, up 5,433.46 points or 3.49% from its previous close of 155,777.21. Analysts at Topline...

item.title: UK government pilots scheme offering up to £40,000 per family to failed asylum seekers for voluntary departure
item.description: The UK Home Office, under Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood, has launched a pilot scheme offering families of failed asylum seekers up to £40,000 in lump-sum payments to encourage voluntary departure fro...

item.title: BARMM Parliament Members Distribute Medicines and Rice to Marginalized Communities for Ramadhan
item.description: Several members of the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) parliament have been actively conducting Ramadhan outreach programmes since the holy month began on February 19, 2026. Th...

item.title: Australian and US markets decline as oil prices spike amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Australian shares are expected to open lower, mirroring losses on Wall Street as oil prices surged past US$83 a barrel. The rise in oil has heightened inflation concerns globally, with geopolitical te...

item.title: Karnataka Cyber Command to Launch Special Wing for Monitoring Child Abuse Material Online
item.description: In a significant move to strengthen online child protection, the Karnataka Cyber Command Unit (CCU) is planning to establish a dedicated wing focused on monitoring and investigating cases involving Ch...

item.title: Digitisation alone cannot solve South Africa's university overcrowding
item.description: Mlamuli Hlatshwayo discusses the limitations of digitising higher education as a solution to the growing demand for university access in South Africa. Despite the increasing uptake of digital learning...

item.title: Diplomatic Deal Enables Limited Evacuation of Australians from the Gulf Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: A delicate diplomatic agreement has been reached to allow some Australians stranded in the Gulf region to return home amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. More than 100,000 Australians, includ...

item.title: Duterte Remains in ICC Custody as Judges Deliberate on Crimes Against Humanity Case
item.description: Former President Rodrigo Duterte is still in detention at the International Criminal Court (ICC) in The Hague, The Netherlands, as the court's judges deliberate whether there is enough evidence to pro...

item.title: Young Africans Secure 3-0 Victory Over Singida Black Stars to Extend Unbeaten League Run
item.description: Defending champions Young Africans (Yanga) continued their dominant form in the Tanzania Mainland Premier League with a convincing 3-0 win against Singida Black Stars at Airtel Stadium on March 5, 202...

item.title: CV Ananda Bose resigns as West Bengal Governor before Assembly polls; RN Ravi likely successor
item.description: West Bengal Governor CV Ananda Bose has resigned from his post, submitting his resignation to President Droupadi Murmu. Bose, who took office in November 2022, reportedly told PTI that he felt he had ...
